senses.gloss: new, novel, recent, late, modern, upstart

examples.text: مَا يَأْتِيهِم مِّن ذِكْرٍ مِّن رَّبِّهِم مُّحْدَثٍ إِلَّا اسْتَمَعُوهُ وَهُمْ يَلْعَبُونَ
examples.english: Whatever new reminder comes to them from their Lord, they only listen to it jokingly.

etymology.text:
Derived from the active participle of the verb حَدَّثَ (ḥaddaṯa).

Derived from the passive participle of أَحْدَثَ (ʔaḥdaṯa).
